cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure this out. The question is about the lines of the film "Elephant" and which option correctly describes how they were created. The student actors made up their own dialogue, with Van Sant asking them to base their characters on their own lives. So the key points here are that the lines weren't pre-written by the director or others. Instead, the actors themselves created the dialogue. Looking at the options: A) created by student actors – this matches the text saying the actors made up their own dialogue. B) given by the director – but the text says the director had no ready-made lines, so this is probably incorrect. C) carefully designed by professional people – the text doesn't mention professionals designing the lines, and it's student actors creating their own. D) thought out by the director during the acting – again, the director didn't have ready-made lines, so this doesn't fit. The lines were created by the actors, not the director during filming.
